qa_pairs.question: What was the initial operating capacity of the Niagara cluster starting January 6, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Beginning January 6, 2025, the Niagara cluster operated at approximately 50% capacity.

qa_pairs.question: What was the initial operating capacity of the Mist cluster starting January 6, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Beginning January 6, 2025, the Mist cluster operated at approximately 35% capacity.

qa_pairs.question: Why were the Niagara and Mist clusters operating at reduced capacities starting January 6, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The reduction in capacity was to support ongoing system improvements and the transition to the new Trillium system.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Mist cluster briefly shut down during the initial reduction period?
qa_pairs.answer: Mist required a brief temporary shutdown on January 6, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When was Niagara's compute node capacity reduced to 863?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara's compute node capacity was reduced to 863 on September 4, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to Niagara Open OnDemand on September 9, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara Open OnDemand was decommissioned on September 9, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What caused a brief interruption on September 9, 2025, affecting Niagara's capacity?
qa_pairs.answer: A data-centre firewall upgrade caused a brief interruption on September 9, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was Niagara's end-of-day capacity on September 9, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara's end-of-day capacity was 647 nodes on September 9, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When was Trillium Open OnDemand launched?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium Open OnDemand was launched on September 11, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What is the URL for Trillium Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: The URL for Trillium Open OnDemand is https://ondemand.scinet.utoronto.ca.

qa_pairs.question: When was Mist decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: Mist was decommissioned on September 16, 2025, during a full-day maintenance event.

qa_pairs.question: What was Niagara's compute node capacity reduced to on September 16, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara was reduced to 431 nodes on September 16, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When was Niagara's compute node capacity reduced to 215?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara's compute node capacity was reduced to 215 compute nodes on September 24, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Niagara cluster fully decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: The Niagara cluster was fully decommissioned on September 30, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the duration of the Niagara and Mist reduction period, including decommissioning?
qa_pairs.answer: The Niagara and Mist reduction period, including decommissioning, lasted 268 days, from January 6, 2025, to September 30, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the operating capacity of the Graham compute cluster starting February 25, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Beginning February 25, 2025, the Graham compute cluster operated at approximately 25% capacity.

qa_pairs.question: Why was the Graham cluster operating at reduced capacity?
qa_pairs.answer: The Graham cluster operated at reduced capacity while data migration and system transition work were underway ahead of its retirement.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Graham compute cluster fully decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham was fully decommissioned on September 1, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Why was the start of the Graham reduction postponed from January to February?
qa_pairs.answer: The start of the reduction was postponed from January to February while storage migration progressed.

qa_pairs.question: Were user logins and storage accessible on Graham during the reduction period?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, user logins remained available and storage was accessible, though project storage was temporarily read-only during migration.

qa_pairs.question: What types of jobs did Graham support during its reduction period?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham supported CPU and GPU jobs, including V100, T4, A100, and A5000, under a simplified scheduling configuration with limited runtimes.

qa_pairs.question: Did Graham Cloud remain operational during the reduction period?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Graham Cloud remained fully operational throughout the reduction period.

qa_pairs.question: What was the duration of the Graham reduction event?
qa_pairs.answer: The Graham reduction event lasted 188 days, from February 25, 2025, to September 1, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What service was unavailable on Béluga, Narval, and Juno between July 15 and August 25, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The tape storage system behind the TSM service, including backups and migrated /nearline data, was unavailable.

qa_pairs.question: Why was the tape storage system unavailable on Béluga, Narval, and Juno?
qa_pairs.answer: It was unavailable during its migration to a new data centre.

qa_pairs.question: What services were specifically unavailable due to the tape storage system migration?
qa_pairs.answer: File backup and restore services were unavailable, and the TSM service itself was fully unavailable.

qa_pairs.question: What impact did the tape storage migration have on data created between July 15 and August 11, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Data created or modified between July 15 and August 11, 2025, could not be recovered.

qa_pairs.question: Were /nearline files migrated to tape accessible on Béluga and Narval during the outage?
qa_pairs.answer: No, files in /nearline that had been migrated to tape were not accessible on Béluga and Narval.

qa_pairs.question: Which systems remained fully operational during the Béluga, Narval, and Juno tape storage outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Other storage systems, compute nodes on all clusters, and Juno Cloud instances remained fully operational.

qa_pairs.question: Did Globus transfers function normally during the tape storage outage on Béluga, Narval, and Juno?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus transfers functioned normally except when accessing tape-migrated /nearline files.

qa_pairs.question: What was the duration of the Béluga, Narval, and Juno tape storage outage?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage lasted 55 days, from July 15, 2025, to September 8, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Why were some compute nodes on Fir temporarily unavailable starting August 25, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Some compute nodes on Fir were temporarily unavailable to support the commissioning of new cooling equipment.

qa_pairs.question: What was the impact on jobs running on Fir during the reduction period?
qa_pairs.answer: Fewer jobs ran concurrently, jobs often started more slowly, and wait times were longer on Fir.

qa_pairs.question: What was the duration of the Fir reduction event?
qa_pairs.answer: The Fir reduction event lasted 12 days, from August 25, 2025, to September 5, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Rorqual compute cluster fully unavailable?
qa_pairs.answer: The Rorqual compute cluster was fully unavailable on September 3, 2025, from 8:00 a.m. (EDT) until 5:00 p.m. (EDT).

qa_pairs.question: Why was the Rorqual cluster unavailable on September 3, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: It was unavailable for scheduled maintenance.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to jobs scheduled to complete after 8:00 a.m. (EDT) on September 3, 2025, on Rorqual?
qa_pairs.answer: Jobs scheduled to complete after 8:00 a.m. (EDT) on September 3 remained in the queue until service returned.

qa_pairs.question: Which systems were not affected by the Rorqual outage on September 3, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Narval cluster and cloud instances on the Béluga and Juno platforms were not affected.
